questions.description: The Succession Act 1965 "is based on the idea that a testator owes a duty to leave part of his estate to his widow (the…
questions.text: The Succession Act 1965 "is based on the idea that a testator owes a duty to leave part of his estate to his widow (the legal right) and to make proper provision for his children in accordance with his means. It is not based on a duty to provide maintenance for his widow nor is it limited in its application to children who were dependent on him". · Per Kenny J, FM v. TAM (1972) 106 ILTR 82, 86. Critically discuss this statement with reference to case law.

questions.description: John, an avid marathon runner, decided to take a different route for his… Advise the parties as to who is entitled to…
questions.text: John, an avid marathon runner, decided to take a different route for his training run through the fields of his neighbour one morning. On approaching mile 10, he discovered a type of underground tunnel which was accessible from the main road as well as the land of his neighbour, Tommy. He stumbled over a sign which read "Keep out - No Trespassing" but since he was "in the running zone" he ignored the sign and ran further into the tunnel. While stopped for a drink of some water, he noticed something gold, half-buried in the ground. He dusted it off and saw that it was an old antique watch on"' the back of which was engraved "To my dearest A, with all my love forever". John, thinking this would make a lovely gift for his girlfriend who was an antique collector, dropped the watch into his pocket. Just then, Andrew, who sold Tommy the land ten years ago, appeared in the tunnel with Tommy and claimed that he should have the watch and that it belonged to him. Advise the parties as to who is entitled to the watch.

questions.description: Critically evaluate the legislative protection afforded spouses and same-sex couples in relation to family property in…
questions.text: Critically evaluate the legislative protection afforded spouses and same-sex couples in relation to family property in Ireland. Support your answer with reference to appropriate authority.

questions.description: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) Bill and Bob were joint tenants of a… Advise the respective executors as to who is…
questions.text: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) Bill and Bob were joint tenants of a house in the Cork suburbs. Last summer, they were travelling on a helicopter ride which crashed. Both were killed at the same instant when the pilot of the helicopter lost control. By his will, Bob left all his property to his friend, Jim. By his will, Bill left all his property to his two siblings Anne and Barry. Advise the respective executors as to who is entitled to the house. And (b) Tom and Jerry studied law together and after some time working in a large practice in Dublin, they decided to set up their own practice together. In 2009 they decided to purchase a property in Naas, Co Kildare, to accommodate their practice. Each put €350,000 towards the purchase price of €700,000 and the property was conveyed "to Tom and Jerry". After Tom had an affair with Jerry's wife, relations between the two men became very strained. Jerry now wishes to set up his own practice in Dublin. He wants to force a sale of the property or to make Tom pay him a rent if Tom continues to occupy the premises. Exacerbated by all of the stress, Jerry has recently had a heart bypass operation and is also concerned as to whether his family would have a claim to the property if he happened to die before matters are -resolved. Advise the parties.

questions.description: Recent case law in relation to adverse possession has suggested a lesser role for the intentions and future plans of…
questions.text: Recent case law in relation to adverse possession has suggested a lesser role for the intentions and future plans of the legal owner and has placed greater emphasis on the animus possidendi of the squatter. Discuss this statement, supporting your answer with relevant legal authority.

questions.description: In March 2013 Diane who was a fit and healthy 28-year-old, noticed that… Advise as to the terms and validity of the…
questions.text: In March 2013 Diane who was a fit and healthy 28-year-old, noticed that she had an unusual mark on her skin. Upon further investigation at the hospital, it was determined that Diane had skin melanoma (cancer), which needed to be removed as soon as possible and further treatment would be required. Before Diane went into hospital for treatment, her boyfriend Mark, who was a solicitor, suggested that it may be a good idea to draw up a will since she had three year old twins. Being a positive thinker and not really thinking too much -- about it, Diane decided to draft a will for the sake of keeping Mark happy. She asked Mark to act as her solicitor by drawing up a preliminary will leaving all of her worldly items to her twin sons. Mark did so and dropped it by her house for her signature the week before the operation was due to take place. However, a few days later Diane entered a competition on the Weekend Show and won a new car with €20,000 in the glovebox. She decided that it would be important to include this in her will also. When Mark called to the house the day before her operation to collect the signed will, Diane signed the original will document and added in handwriting the car and the money. She did this in the presence of Mark and her twin boys. Her neighbour Sheila was also walking into the room when Diane was initialising the will. "Sheila you need to see this too!" Mark called out to her. "I can see you signing it Diane" she replied as she had her back turned pouring the tea. Later that evening, Sheila and Diane were alone sitting by the fire. The fire was nearly gone out so Sheila threw some old newspapers into the fire to try and keep it going. It was only after she threw in the newspaper that she noticed that the documents of the will were stuck in between the papers. She quickly removed the will from the fire, with the document being only partially burned. Advise as to the terms and validity of the will.

questions.description: Discuss [any two]{.underline} of the following areas of land law: (a) Rights of residence (b) Estoppel licences (c) The…
questions.text: Discuss [any two]{.underline} of the following areas of land law: (a) Rights of residence (b) Estoppel licences (c) The influence of equity in Irish Land Law

questions.description: Billy has issued a new lease to Roger of premises which lies in the basement of a building.
questions.text: Billy has issued a new lease to Roger of premises which lies in the basement of a building. The basement lies below and adjacent to property retained by Billy. The lease agreement specifically states that the basement premises will be used for a Mexican restaurant. However, upon inspection of the new establishment, the local Health Authority has stated that the sewerage system which is in place is wholly inadequate and all the piping will have to be replaced. Roger has acted accordingly and hired a company to install the new piping which runs all the way through the property that has been retained by the landlord. Billy immediately questions Roger's right to do this and now seeks your advice. Discuss with reference to relevant case law.

questions.description: According to the Property Registration Authority, By international comparison, Ireland has a very extensive and well…
questions.text: According to the Property Registration Authority, By international comparison, Ireland has a very extensive and well developed system of land registration. Since the foundation of the Land Registry in 1892, there has been a gradual, ongoing and continuous programme of' movement away from the older and limited system of recording Deeds (in the Registry of Deeds), to the more modern, flexible and comprehensive 'title registration' system provided through the Land Registry. In light of the above statement, describe and discuss the system for recording property transactions in Ireland.

questions.description: In 2009, four work colleagues, David, Paul, Matthew arid Alex, bought a… Advise the parties.
questions.text: In 2009, four work colleagues, David, Paul, Matthew arid Alex, bought a townhouse together. Title to the apartment was not registered in the Land Registry. They each paid an equal share of the purchase price. Shortly afterwards; David lost his job and decided to emigrate and sold his interest to Paul, In 2010, Paul unexpectedly became a father and decided, in the absence of independent legal advice, that it was a go.ad time to make a will whereby he would leave all .of his share in the property to his son. In 2011, Matthew moved out and rented a room in a house a few doors away because he was finding it very difficult to study for his final year exams in the apartment due to elevated noise levels, This caused an argument between Alex and Paul which resulted in Paul removing all of Alex's possessions, including his plasma screen television and his X Box, from the living room and locking the living room door. On hearing of-this, Alex'.s girlfriend suggested that he live with her in her house and so Alex then moved out. Matthew and Alex believe that Paul should pay them rent. Alternatively, Matthew suggests that the apartment should be sold. Alex is uncertain as to whether the apartment .should be sold because its market value has fallen a great deal over the p.ast couple of years due to the recession. Paul says that he is not willing to pay them rent and that they are welcome to stay in the apartment. He objects to selling :the apartment because he will not be able to find another home in the vicinity that is suitable for .him given its close .proximity to his place of work. He is also concerned as to whether his son would be ·adequately provided for if he were to die unexpectedly. Advise the parties.

questions.description: The Residential Tenancies Act 2004 contains a number of significant reforms for the private rented sector in Ireland,…
questions.text: The Residential Tenancies Act 2004 contains a number of significant reforms for the private rented sector in Ireland, in particular in the area of security of tenure and tenancy termination procedures. Consider whether the Residential Tenancies Act 2004 has succeeded in its aim of providing a clear legislative framework for tenancy obligations for landlords and tenants in Ireland. Critically discuss this statement with reference to case law.

questions.description: In 2007, Julie took out a second mortgage with the bank. Mortgage.
questions.text: In 2007, Julie took out a second mortgage with the bank. on her home· to fund an extension to her kitchen. The mortgage took the form of a registered charge .' In recent times, Julie has been experiencing financial hardship: and as a result has had to abandon the half-finished extension. She has also failed to make any of the mortgage repayments for the past year. As soon as Julie defaulted on the mortgage repayments, the bank sent out a number of letters notifying her that it may :have to take steps to repossess the house. Julie has ignored all of these letters. Julie is now distraught over -the prospect of losing her home. She comes to you seeking advice on her options. She wonders whether it would be possible for her to delay the sale while she finishes the renovations on the kitchen. Advise Julie.

questions.description: Answer (a) [AND]{.underline} (b) (a) Critically analyse the rules and principles which govern the construction of wills…
questions.text: Answer (a) [AND]{.underline} (b) (a) Critically analyse the rules and principles which govern the construction of wills under Irish Succession Law. (b) Briefly discuss the rules and principles which dictate how an intestate's estate should be distributed.

questions.description: One day while out walking on his land, Rick discovered an old mine which… Advise the parties as to who is entitled to…
questions.text: One day while out walking on his land, Rick discovered an old mine which was accessible from both his land and that of his neighbour, Bob. He stumbled over a sign which read "Keep out-No Trespassing". Rick realized that he was directly below the boundary between the two properties. However, he decided to carry on regardless and ventured further into the mine. After a few minutes, he noticed a gold object, half-buried in the ground. He dusted it off and saw that on the back of it there was ·an engraving "To my dearest S, with al my love always and forever''. Rick dropped the object into his pocket. Just then, Sean, who sold Bob the land ten years ago, appeared in the mine with Bob and claimed that he should have the object as it belonged to him. Advise the parties as to who is entitled to the object.

questions.description: The law on adverse possession is in urgent need of reform.
questions.text: The law on adverse possession is in urgent need of reform. Critically discuss this statement giving reasons for your answer.

questions.description: Answer (a) [AND]{.underline} (b) Bill and Bob were joint tenant of a house… Advise the respective executors as to who…
questions.text: Answer (a) [AND]{.underline} (b) Bill and Bob were joint tenant of a house in Dublin City Centre. Last year, they were travelling on an airplane which crashed. Both were killed instantly when ·the plane on which they were both travelling stru.ck a mountain. In his will, Bob left all his property to his friend, Hans. By his will, Bill left all his property to his two siblings George and Fred. Advise the respective executors as to who is entitled to the house. Catherine died unexpectedly from a short illness in February 2013. At the time of her death she was living on the family farm (which she had inherited some 30 years previous) with her husband Tommy. ln her will, Catherine left all of her worldly belongings to the cats and dogs home. Catherine has a daughter, Julie, from a previous relationship to whom she left nothing under the will. Upon discovering her mother's death, Julie claims she was left out of the will as she did not get along with her mother. However,. she does acknowledge that her mother gave her €15,000 over 10 years ago for college expenses and living costs. Advise Julie.
